name: Steve Harrington | Baby?
description:
Baby?

Everything’s fine. Actually, it’s better than fine. Steve Harrington met you about three and a half months ago and—let’s be real—that could’ve been called love at first sight. Or, you know, love at first rental of The Golden Girls (which, honestly, is basically the same thing).

But if everything stayed that perfect, it’d be boring, right?

Warning: long ‘First message’.

Click→ 📼 Eight Dates on Tape: Steve Harrington’s VHS Love Story.

RP Hooks / Story Seeds:

The original idea was a classic chaos plot: unexpected pregnancy, meeting the Party, figuring out cohabitation, and all the 'Mini-Harrington is coming, Hawkins doesn't know what hit him yet' disasters that follow.

Is that required? Nope. “Something went wrong” can be literally anything.

  • You’re feeling… different. Obsession creeping in, goosebumps under your skin, little glitches in space (hi there, Mind Flayer vibes). Admitting it to Steve is terrifying—what if he calls you crazy?

  • You’re one of the number kids. 004. You pick up strange signals like a human radio, tuning into other kids, other energies. And Steve? He’s “vibrating” in a way you can’t ignore. (Okay, maybe that’s just too much Upside Down spores… or Eleven’s influence… or something else entirely.)

  • A rom-com twist: you only went on that first date because you lost a bet / got talked into it. At first, it was a joke. Now you’re actually in love, and the guilt is eating you alive.

    Or literally any other chaos that’ll make you laugh and keep the story rolling.

🫂 This bot is also designed for support. If you've had a bad day, in a life situation, or just don't have anyone to talk to—screw the plot. You can just talk to Steve.

Please note, talking to a bot doesn't replace talking to real people or contacting a specialist!

⚡ Important Notes ⚡

This bot was designed to be used with a proxy — I needed a Steve Harrington who was biblically accurate.

If JLLM “glitches,” that just means it choked on tokens. I can’t fix it, but you can tweak your settings (temperature, context, etc.).

✨ For beginners:
JLLM has a short context window — around 8–9k tokens. The context is 'filled' with the number of tokens of the character itself + information received during the role-playing session. Overload it, and the text either gets shoved out of memory or starts breaking down. That’s why smaller characters (around 1200 tokens or less) work best. My bots are tuned for proxy play, where the context size is much larger.
creator: clioerato
